entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official Hermes Agent memory provider, Mem0 offers a universal layer that manages multi-level retention across users, sessions, and agent states. It utilizes temporal reasoning and a hybrid retrieval system—combining semantic, BM25, and entity linking—to ensure time-aware ranking of past and future events.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'agent brain' features a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts entity relationships without requiring expensive LLM calls. It enhances context retrieval by combining vector search with backlink-boosted graph ranking, supporting 14 different embedding providers including local Ollama instances.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'learn-as-you-go' architecture through specialized retain, recall, and reflect workflows designed for long-term agent evolution. It provides an enterprise-grade solution with a two-line LLM wrapper that automates memory management using PostgreSQL or Oracle AI Database backends.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that uses asynchronous reasoning to extract conclusions from message traces and event logs. Its peer-centric model tracks evolving entities and multi-peer perspectives, utilizing hybrid BM25 and vector search to maintain complex context.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness employs five specialized agent roles to create an iterative self-improvement loop for complex tasks. It generates structured playbooks and persistent knowledge traces, allowing Hermes Agents to refine their own operational context over time.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A Lossless Context Management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG structure to compact conversations without losing raw message history. It includes dedicated agent tools for grepping and expanding compacted context, backed by a persistent SQLite store.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library, enabling private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent deployments. It features an automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis, supporting any OpenAI-compatible or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP or agent plugins, utilizing a triple-threat retrieval system of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs for on-device privacy.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on inference efficiency, ContextPilot reduces prompt tokens by up to 36% through aggressive context deduplication. It boosts prefill speeds for Hermes Agent and vLLM by optimizing prefix sharing, ensuring accurate memory retrieval with lower computational overhead.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format to facilitate zero-cost local storage. It applies ACT-R cognitive principles to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information, providing MCP tools for automated learning in multi-agent systems.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases, documents, and media with automatic OCR and summarization. It uses bidirectional edge tags to create navigable relationships between notes, making it an ideal tool-integrated memory for agents via MCP or CLI.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system designed for sub-millisecond latency in read, write, and search operations. Built specifically as a Hermes Agent plugin, it leverages SQLite with native vector and full-text search for high-performance local storage.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: This memory kernel consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval into a single stack for Hermes Agents. Its unique 'five-shelf' architecture prevents memory from becoming a flat, unorganized blob, ensuring structured data retrieval.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. It utilizes a local-first architecture with SQLite-vec and GGUF models, offering an MCP-native server for seamless integration with coding agent clients.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This project provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It uses the agentskills.io format to enable sub-second discovery and execution across 26 specialized security domains.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw automates the deduplication and quality improvement of skills across different agent frameworks and devices. It allows user groups to share learned skills, enabling a collective evolution of agent capabilities over time.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It utilizes a two-pass audit system and a comprehensive word replacement table to ensure consistent, human-like output.
entries.wondelai/skills: This is a modular, cross-platform library compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io agents. It offers a marketplace of skills covering diverse professional fields including architecture, UX design, and product strategy.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This implementation of the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI uses progressive disclosure to reduce token usage. It supports both programmatic Python definitions and filesystem-based skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables agents to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language, exporting them to formats like XML, SVG, or PDF. It includes specialized presets for UML and architecture diagrams alongside an iterative self-check refinement loop.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches agents to write their own analytical prompts and identify structural conservation laws within complex codebases. It generates constraint reports to highlight analysis blind spots and limitations during problem-solving.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracle networks, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes CLI tools for local simulations and monitoring cross-chain smart contract workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill implements literate programming by weaving runnable source code and prose into unified .lit.md files. It features a tangler to extract code for execution and automatically syncs edits back to the primary documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus facilitates cross-instance shared memory and the automated extraction of training data from agent workflows. It is designed to help agents 'train their replacement' through a pipeline of decision-quality tagging and model fine-tuning.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock.io is a registry for reusable AI skills that features recursive dependency resolution and an OpenAPI-driven SDK. It uniquely integrates a commerce flow supporting both Stripe and the TON blockchain for skill transactions.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation models. They support precise technical controls such as hex color specification and JSON structured prompting.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both the necessary Python scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation to quickly expand an agent's toolkit.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness for long-running agents that manages state through JSON snapshots and structured mission lifecycles. It facilitates complex tasks using a plan-approve-execute flow and generates markdown briefs for session handoffs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and real-time progress tracking. It ensures operational safety by providing rollback procedures for recovery if a task fails.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize token consumption when handling large toolsets. It utilizes a socket-based daemon to maintain persistent connections across multiple Model Context Protocol servers.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures high reliability by providing verifiable audit trails with line-level citations for all generated skills.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust scores for different skill creators.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and automatic registration of skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill allows Hermes to manage Nextcloud files, calendars, and contacts using standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It features a guided setup script for secure configuration via Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of Nix packages for AI coding agents and CLI tools, including Hermes, with automated daily updates for the latest software versions. It is ideal for users seeking a wide variety of Nix-managed orchestrators, proxies, and terminal-based assistants in a single repository.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw offers a highly secure environment for Hermes Agent using hardware-level enforcement via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level security layers. It features dynamic policy presets for granular control over filesystem and network access, alongside 40+ built-in skills for automated workflows.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository enables reproducible Hermes deployments through a dedicated NixOS module that manages configurations, documents, and systemd services declaratively. It prioritizes security with sops-nix integration for secrets handling and supports a hybrid model for both Nix-managed and interactive tool installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent provides a GUI and 100 built-in tools without requiring Docker or complex setups. It supports local model execution via LM Studio and includes a dynamic tool maker for building custom automation pipelines.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It allows for persistent state through mounted directories and supports building specific Hermes versions using Docker build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes instances with distinct identities and workspaces from a single web application. It facilitates complex operations through hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and enterprise-grade MCP access for external integrations.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for Ubuntu and Debian servers. It is optimized for headless operation and the autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs via Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal interface for easy browser-based access. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users who require cost monitoring and a human-in-the-loop 'Aegis' system for manual task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with hierarchical delegation and durable memory across more than 23 model providers. It stands out by allowing Hermes-based agents to learn new skills directly from conversation history while maintaining complex organizational structures.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework utilizes an automated outer-loop search process to optimize the harness code used for evaluating Hermes Agent performance. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent and Paperclip, vie for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains to handle complex software engineering tasks like research, planning, and infrastructure. It enforces a strict pipeline architecture with mandatory security audits and quality gates for every code change.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase process that uses graph-diff phase gates to ensure architectural integrity from planning to final health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation framework routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution through isolated workflows. It prioritizes operational safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits on all delegated actions.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI provides local data visualization for tracking token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding environments. It is essential for Hermes developers who need to monitor resource usage alongside other agents like Claude Code and Gemini.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files. It supports custom ASCII banners and spinner animations, offering a way to create and share unique visual themes while inheriting default system behaviors.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for a native, process-free experience. It eliminates the need for OAuth and external processes while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, this tool exposes web navigation to agents via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features a supervisory UI with annotated checkpoints, allowing developers to audit and supervise Hermes Agent as it performs browser-based tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders. It allows for seamless switching between default and local agents, each with its own project-specific memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that performs deterministic scans of agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs. It helps developers maintain high-quality Hermes implementations by detecting seven specific structural failure modes, including H1-H7 patterns.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It ensures a smooth onboarding process by handling the transfer of complex model configurations and local credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, enabling a zero-cost agent environment in minutes.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A feature-complete web interface that mirrors the Hermes CLI experience via a three-panel layout. It includes a dedicated workspace file browser and a visual context ring for real-time token monitoring.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A multi-platform dashboard focused on connectivity and analytics. It provides unified configuration for channels like Telegram and Discord alongside detailed tracking of token costs and model distribution.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace featuring a memory browser and a built-in terminal. It introduces Swarm Mode for role-based dispatch of multiple persistent agents with zero-fork integration.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted management gateway for the Hermes stack. It combines a split-view file editor and browser-based terminal with a control panel for starting, stopping, and configuring individual agent profiles.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that connects directly to remote Hermes hosts via SSH. It replaces middleman APIs with a local interface for Kanban boards, session data, and remote file management.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed specifically for Telegram Mini App v2.0. It features a terminal-style chat interface and system monitoring for CPU, memory, and disk usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no Docker or admin rights. It bundles over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and TTS, and includes a dynamic tool maker for runtime API wrappers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A companion app that simplifies the setup process through guided installation and dependency resolution. It supports 14 specialized toolsets and integrated gateway management for various chat platforms.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-centric dashboard focused on service health and session activity. It offers full-text search for conversation histories and management for scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace featuring tool approval cards and real-time SSE streaming. It includes an integrated marketplace for MCP servers and skills, with isolated profile management for separate API keys.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic React interface built on a single-file architecture. It provides real-time log streaming, inline workspace previews, and management tools for MCP skills and cron jobs.
